quizQuestion: On which platform did MrBeast become the most-subscribed individual creator?
explanation: Jimmy 'MrBeast' Donaldson became the most-subscribed individual on YouTube, known for expensive challenge videos and large-scale philanthropy. He started his channel in 2012 at age 13. Source: Wikipedia/MrBeast

quizQuestion: In what year did MrBeast (Jimmy Donaldson) start his YouTube channel?
explanation: MrBeast launched his YouTube channel in 2012 at age 13, spending years experimenting with content before his viral breakthrough. He grew into the most-subscribed individual creator on the platform. Source: Wikipedia/MrBeast

quizQuestion: What nationality is Felix Kjellberg, better known as PewDiePie?
explanation: Felix Kjellberg, known online as PewDiePie, is Swedish. He was the first individual creator to reach 100 million YouTube subscribers, achieving that milestone in August 2019. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

quizQuestion: In what year did PewDiePie become the first individual to reach 100 million YouTube subscribers?
explanation: PewDiePie (Felix Kjellberg) crossed 100 million YouTube subscribers in August 2019, becoming the first individual creator to reach that milestone. He held the most-subscribed crown for years before being overtaken. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

quizQuestion: What is PewDiePie's real name?
explanation: PewDiePie's real name is Felix Arvid Ulf Kjellberg. The Swedish YouTuber rose to fame primarily through Let's Play videos and became one of the most recognizable figures in online entertainment. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

quizQuestion: How many seasons did The Oprah Winfrey Show run before ending in 2011?
explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show ran for 25 seasons from 1986 to 2011. It became the highest-rated daytime talk show in history, and 'Oprah's Book Club' could instantly propel any selected title to bestseller status. Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

quizQuestion: In what year did The Oprah Winfrey Show premiere nationally?
explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show premiered nationally in 1986 and ran until 2011, totaling 25 seasons. It became the highest-rated daytime talk show in television history. Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

quizQuestion: Which daytime talk show, running 25 seasons from 1986 to 2011, is considered the highest-rated in its genre's history?
explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show ran from 1986 to 2011 for 25 seasons and became the highest-rated daytime talk show in history. Oprah's endorsements and book club picks were so powerful they created the 'Oprah Effect.' Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

quizQuestion: In what year was San Diego Comic-Con founded?
explanation: San Diego Comic-Con was founded in 1970, originally as a small comic book convention. It has since evolved into the world's premier pop culture event, drawing over 130,000 attendees annually. Source: Wikipedia/San_Diego_Comic-Con

quizQuestion: In which California city is the world's most famous Comic-Con convention held annually?
explanation: San Diego Comic-Con, founded in 1970, draws over 130,000 attendees annually and has evolved from a comic book convention into the world's premier pop culture event for films, TV, games, and collectibles. Source: Wikipedia/San_Diego_Comic-Con

quizQuestion: Who coined the term 'cosplay' in 1984?
explanation: Japanese reporter Nobuyuki Takahashi coined the term 'cosplay' in 1984 as a portmanteau of 'costume play' after attending the 1984 World Science Fiction Convention (Worldcon) in Los Angeles. Source: Wikipedia/Cosplay

quizQuestion: In what year was the term 'cosplay' first coined?
explanation: The term 'cosplay' was coined in 1984 by Japanese reporter Nobuyuki Takahashi. It is a portmanteau of 'costume play' and has grown into a global subculture with multi-billion dollar convention and merchandise economies. Source: Wikipedia/Cosplay

quizQuestion: In what year did 'The Dress' viral phenomenon generate 2.2 million tweets in 24 hours over its ambiguous colors?
explanation: The Dress went viral on February 25, 2015, generating 2.2 million tweets in 24 hours. The photo of a blue-and-black dress sparked mainstream media discussion about human color perception and optical illusions. Source: Wikipedia/The_dress_(viral_phenomenon)

quizQuestion: What were the actual colors of 'The Dress,' the 2015 viral photo that divided the internet over its appearance?
explanation: The Dress that went viral in 2015 was actually blue and black, though many viewers perceived it as white and gold. The phenomenon sparked widespread discussion about color perception, lighting, and individual differences in human vision. Source: Wikipedia/The_dress_(viral_phenomenon)

quizQuestion: Which 2013 Netflix original series pioneered the binge-watching model by dropping an entire season at once?
explanation: Netflix pioneered binge-watching in February 2013 by releasing all 13 episodes of House of Cards simultaneously. This model disrupted traditional TV broadcasting and became Netflix's defining strategy for original content. Source: Wikipedia/Netflix

quizQuestion: In what year did Netflix pioneer the binge-watching model by releasing all episodes of House of Cards at once?
explanation: In February 2013, Netflix released all 13 episodes of House of Cards simultaneously, establishing the binge-watching model that would define streaming culture. Netflix surpassed cable TV subscriber counts in 2017. Source: Wikipedia/Netflix

quizQuestion: Which streaming service pioneered the binge-release model in 2013 with House of Cards?
explanation: Netflix pioneered the all-at-once episode release model in February 2013 with House of Cards, transforming how audiences consume scripted television. Netflix surpassed cable TV in total U.S. subscribers in 2017. Source: Wikipedia/Netflix

quizQuestion: In what year did Disney+ launch and reach 10 million subscribers in a single day?
explanation: Disney+ launched on November 12, 2019 and reached 10 million subscribers in just one day — faster than any prior streaming service had achieved. The platform was bolstered by Marvel, Star Wars, and Disney animated content. Source: Wikipedia/Disney+

quizQuestion: How many subscribers did Disney+ acquire on its launch day in November 2019?
explanation: Disney+ reached 10 million subscribers on its launch day of November 12, 2019 — faster than any prior streaming service. The milestone demonstrated massive pent-up demand for Disney, Marvel, and Star Wars content in one place. Source: Wikipedia/Disney+

quizQuestion: Which streaming platform launched in 2019 and signed up 10 million subscribers on its very first day?
explanation: Disney+ launched November 12, 2019 and amassed 10 million subscribers in a single day — the fastest any streaming service had reached that mark. It served as the home for Marvel, Star Wars, Disney animated films, and Pixar. Source: Wikipedia/Disney+

quizQuestion: In what year was E3, the Electronic Entertainment Expo, first held?
explanation: E3 (Electronic Entertainment Expo) was first held in 1995 and became the world's biggest gaming event for nearly three decades. Publishers used it to announce major titles and hardware before a global audience. Source: Wikipedia/E3

quizQuestion: In what year did E3, once the world's biggest gaming expo, permanently shut down after nearly 30 years?
explanation: E3 was permanently canceled in 2023, ending a nearly 30-year run as the video game industry's premier showcase. Major publishers had already begun withdrawing years earlier, and the COVID-19 pandemic accelerated its decline. Source: Wikipedia/E3

quizQuestion: In what year did the podcast 'Serial' debut and spark the true crime podcast boom?
explanation: Serial debuted in 2014 and amassed over 80 million downloads, becoming a cultural phenomenon that helped establish podcasting as a mainstream medium and launched the true crime podcast genre into the spotlight. Source: Wikipedia/Serial_(podcast)

quizQuestion: What audio format did 'Serial' help push into the mainstream when it debuted in 2014 with over 80 million downloads?
explanation: Serial was a podcast that debuted in 2014 and accumulated over 80 million downloads. Its massive success sparked the true crime podcast boom and demonstrated that podcasting could reach mainstream pop culture audiences. Source: Wikipedia/Serial_(podcast)

quizQuestion: Which 2014 podcast amassed over 80 million downloads and is credited with sparking the true crime podcast boom?
explanation: Serial, debuting in 2014, was the first podcast to reach 5 million iTunes downloads. It reinvestigated the 1999 murder of Hae Min Lee and helped transform podcasting from a niche medium into mainstream entertainment. Source: Wikipedia/Serial_(podcast)

quizQuestion: In what year did The Blair Witch Project pioneer viral internet marketing for films by presenting itself as real found footage?
explanation: The Blair Witch Project (1999) pioneered viral internet marketing by creating a website and backstory presenting the film as real found footage of missing student filmmakers. Made for roughly $60,000, it grossed nearly $250 million worldwide. Source: Wikipedia/The_Blair_Witch_Project

quizQuestion: Which 1999 horror film pioneered viral internet marketing by presenting itself as real found footage of missing filmmakers?
explanation: The Blair Witch Project (1999) created a fictional 'mythology' website and presented itself as real found footage. It is widely credited as the first major film to leverage viral internet marketing, grossing nearly $250 million on a roughly $60,000 budget. Source: Wikipedia/The_Blair_Witch_Project

quizQuestion: In what year was Settlers of Catan, the catalyst of the modern board game renaissance, first published?
explanation: Settlers of Catan was first published in 1995 in Germany and became the catalyst for the modern board game renaissance. The global board game market grew from a niche hobby into a $15+ billion industry, fueled largely by Eurogames inspired by Catan. Source: Wikipedia/Catan

quizQuestion: Approximately how large was the global tabletop gaming market by 2023, driven by the Eurogame renaissance?
explanation: The modern board game market reached over $15 billion globally by 2023, driven by the Eurogame renaissance that began with Settlers of Catan in 1995. Hobby game stores, crowdfunding, and conventions like Gen Con accelerated the boom. Source: Wikipedia/Catan

quizQuestion: Which 1995 German strategy board game is widely credited as the catalyst for the modern board game renaissance?
explanation: Settlers of Catan, published in Germany in 1995, introduced American audiences to Eurogames and sparked a massive resurgence in tabletop gaming. It has sold over 45 million copies worldwide and helped grow the board game market into a $15+ billion global industry. Source: Wikipedia/Catan

quizQuestion: In what year did GamerGate, the online harassment campaign that sparked debate about diversity in gaming, occur?
explanation: GamerGate began in 2014 as an online harassment campaign targeting women in the gaming industry. It sparked a broad cultural debate about diversity, toxicity, and journalistic ethics in gaming culture that continues to influence industry discussions. Source: Wikipedia/Gamergate_(harassment_campaign)

quizQuestion: What is MrBeast's real name?
explanation: MrBeast's real name is Jimmy Donaldson. He began his YouTube channel in 2012 at age 13 and grew it into the most-subscribed individual channel on the platform, known for philanthropy and elaborate challenge videos. Source: Wikipedia/MrBeast

quizQuestion: Which annual convention, founded in 1970, draws over 130,000 attendees and became the world's premier pop culture event?
explanation: San Diego Comic-Con was founded in 1970 as a small comic book convention and grew into the world's most prominent pop culture event. It now attracts over 130,000 attendees and major announcements from Hollywood studios and game publishers. Source: Wikipedia/San_Diego_Comic-Con

quizQuestion: The word 'cosplay' is a portmanteau of what two English words?
explanation: Cosplay is a portmanteau of 'costume' and 'play,' coined in 1984 by Japanese reporter Nobuyuki Takahashi. The term was originally used to describe attendees dressing as science fiction characters and has since grown into a worldwide subculture. Source: Wikipedia/Cosplay

quizQuestion: What does E3 stand for in the now-canceled annual gaming convention that ran from 1995 to 2023?
explanation: E3 stands for Electronic Entertainment Expo. Founded in 1995, it was the world's most prominent video game trade event for nearly 30 years before permanently shutting down in 2023 as publishers shifted to individual online showcases. Source: Wikipedia/E3
